revision.text:
{{Nihongo|'''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858.
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]]<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref>. The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in [[Kanbun]]-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References==
* {{cite book | last = Kubota | first = Jun | title = Iwanami Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | date = 2007 | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 978-4-00-080310-6 }} * {{cite book | last = Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten Henshū Iinkai | title = Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | date = 1986 | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 4-00-080067-1 }}
{{Japan-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku}}
[[Category:Late Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:History books]]
[[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]]

revision.comment: added some basic data about this Japanese emperor
r2.5.2) (robot Adding: [[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]]

clean up, References after punctuation per [[WP:REFPUNC]] and [[WP:PAIC]] using [[Project:AWB|AWB]] (8748)

It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/montoku.html Text of the ''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku''] (Japanese) *Manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0043/ scans], [[Waseda University Library]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/montoku.html Text of the ''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku''] (Japanese) *Manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0043/ scans], [[Waseda University Library]]

{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/montoku.html Text of the ''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku''] (Japanese) *Manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0043/ scans], [[Waseda University Library]]

{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''

{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''

The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''

{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''

{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''

{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/montoku.html Text of the ''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku''] (Japanese) *Manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0043/ scans], [[Waseda University Library]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}

{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''

{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/montoku.html Text of the ''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku''] (Japanese) *Manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0043/ scans], [[Waseda University Library]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}

{{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Montoku Tennó Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[ff:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon Montoku tennō jitsuroku]][[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]][[ko:일본문덕천황실록]][[pt:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1052905]] Wikidata name: Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[:en:History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[:en:Ōe no Otondo|Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[:en:Miyako no Yoshika|Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/montoku.html Text of the ''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku''] (Japanese) *Manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0043/ scans], [[:en:Waseda University Library|Waseda University Library]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}
{{Rikkokushi}}

It was primarily edited by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[:en:Ōe no Otondo|Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[:en:Miyako no Yoshika|Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Montoku Tennó Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[ff:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon Montoku tennō jitsuroku]][[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]][[ko:일본문덕천황실록]][[pt:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Montoku Tennó Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[ff:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon Montoku tennō jitsuroku]][[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]][[ko:일본문덕천황실록]][[pt:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Montoku Tennó Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[ff:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon Montoku tennō jitsuroku]][[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]][[ko:일본문덕천황실록]][[pt:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1052905]] Wikidata name: Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[:en:History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[:en:Ōe no Otondo|Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[:en:Miyako no Yoshika|Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/montoku.html Text of the ''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku''] (Japanese) *Manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0043/ scans], [[:en:Waseda University Library|Waseda University Library]]

Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[:en:Ōe no Otondo|Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[:en:Miyako no Yoshika|Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>

* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Montoku Tennó Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[ff:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon Montoku tennō jitsuroku]][[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]][[ko:일본문덕천황실록]][[pt:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Montoku Tennó Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[ff:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon Montoku tennō jitsuroku]][[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]][[ko:일본문덕천황실록]][[pt:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Montoku Tennó Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[ff:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon Montoku tennō jitsuroku]][[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]][[ko:일본문덕천황실록]][[pt:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1052905]] Wikidata name: Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned [[:en:History of Japan|Japanese history]] text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by [[:en:Fujiwara no Mototsune|Fujiwara no Mototsune]] with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], [[:en:Ōe no Otondo|Ōe no Otondo]], [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], [[:en:Sugawara no Koreyoshi|Sugawara no Koreyoshi]], [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by [[:en:Miyako no Yoshika|Miyako no Yoshika]].<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kanbun|Kanbun]]|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku's]] reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/montoku.html Text of the ''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku''] (Japanese) *Manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0043/ scans], [[:en:Waseda University Library|Waseda University Library]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Montoku Tennó Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[ff:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon Montoku tennō jitsuroku]][[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]][[ko:일본문덕천황실록]][[pt:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Montoku Tennó Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[ff:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon Montoku tennō jitsuroku]][[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]][[ko:일본문덕천황실록]][[pt:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[cs:Nihon Montoku Tennó Džicuroku]][[de:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[es:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[ff:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[fr:Nihon Montoku tennō jitsuroku]][[ja:日本文徳天皇実録]][[ko:일본문덕천황실록]][[pt:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]][[zh:日本文德天皇實錄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1052905]] Wikidata name: Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese history text completed in 879}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'''''|日本文徳天皇実録||"The True History of Emperor Montoku of Japan"}}, abbreviated as '''Montoku Jitsuroku''', is an officially commissioned {{ill|History of Japan|en|History of Japan|WD=Q130436|lt=Japanese history}} text. Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku}} (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa}} ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by {{ill|Fujiwara no Mototsune|en|Fujiwara no Mototsune|WD=Q1397196|lt=Fujiwara no Mototsune}} with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], {{ill|Ōe no Otondo|en|Ōe no Otondo|WD=Q11436894|lt=Ōe no Otondo}}, [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], {{ill|Sugawara no Koreyoshi|en|Sugawara no Koreyoshi|WD=Q11619187|lt=Sugawara no Koreyoshi}}, [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by {{ill|Miyako no Yoshika|en|Miyako no Yoshika|WD=Q11643817|lt=Miyako no Yoshika}}.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|{{ill|Kanbun|en|Kanbun|WD=Q7901319|lt=Kanbun}}|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku's}} reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
==See also==
* ''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories''
==External links== *[http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/montoku.html Text of the ''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku''] (Japanese) *Manuscript [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ri05/ri05_02450/ri05_02450_0043/ scans], {{ill|Waseda University Library|en|Waseda University Library|WD=Q7971387|lt=Waseda University Library}}

Completed in 879, it is the fifth text in the [[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]] series. It covers the years 850-858, the years of reign of the 55th Japanese sovereign, {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku|1=Emperor Montoku}} (827-858).
==Background==
Following the earlier national history ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'' (869), in 871 {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa|1=Emperor Seiwa}} ordered the compilation of the years since then. It was primarily edited by {{ill|Fujiwara no Mototsune|en|Fujiwara no Mototsune|WD=Q1397196|lt=Fujiwara no Mototsune|1=Fujiwara no Mototsune}} with assistance from [[Minabuchi no Toshina]], {{ill|Ōe no Otondo|en|Ōe no Otondo|WD=Q11436894|lt=Ōe no Otondo|1=Ōe no Otondo}}, [[Shimada no Tadaomi]], {{ill|Sugawara no Koreyoshi|en|Sugawara no Koreyoshi|WD=Q11619187|lt=Sugawara no Koreyoshi|1=Sugawara no Koreyoshi}}, [[Yoshibuchi no Yoshinari]], and significant contributions by {{ill|Miyako no Yoshika|en|Miyako no Yoshika|WD=Q11643817|lt=Miyako no Yoshika|1=Miyako no Yoshika}}.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1418)</ref> The text was completed in 879.
==Contents==
Written in {{lang|ja-latn|{{ill|Kanbun|en|Kanbun|WD=Q7901319|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun}}|italic=no}}-style and contained within ten volumes, the contents cover nine years of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku's|1=Emperor Montoku}} reign spanning from 850 through 858. The text is characteristic in that it contains few political details but many obituaries for nobles.<ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten" /><ref name="Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Jiten (2007:348)</ref>
